2605.06663.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
2605.06663.paper_summary: Large language models are typically deployed as monolithic systems, requiring the full model even when applications need only a narrow subset of capabilities, e.g., code, math, or domain-specific knowledge. Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs) seemingly offer a potential alternative by activating only a subset of experts per input, but in practice, restricting inference to a subset of experts for a given domain leads to severe performance degradation. This limits their practicality in memory-constrained settings, especially as models grow larger and sparser. We introduce EMO, an MoE designed for modularity-the independent use and composition of expert subsets-without requiring human-defined priors. Our key idea is to encourage tokens from similar domains to rely on similar experts. Since tokens within a document often share a domain, EMO restricts them to select experts from a shared pool, while allowing different documents to use different pools. This simple constraint enables coherent expert groupings to emerge during pretraining using document boundaries alone. We pretrain a 1B-active, 14B-total EMO on 1T tokens. As a full model, it matches standard MoE performance. Crucially, it enables selective expert use: retaining only 25% (12.5%) of experts incurs just a 1% (3%) absolute drop, whereas standard MoEs break under the same setting. We further find that expert subsets in EMO specialize at semantic levels (e.g., domains such as math or code), in contrast to the low-level syntactic specialization observed in standard MoEs. Altogether, our results demonstrate a path toward modular, memory-efficient deployment of large, sparse models and open new opportunities for composable architectures.
2605.06663.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型通常以單體系統的形式部署，即使應用程序僅需要狹窄的能力子集（例如，代碼、數學或特定領域知識），也需要完整的模型。專家混合（MoEs）似乎提供了一種潛在的替代方案，通過每個輸入僅激活一部分專家，但在實踐中，將推理限制在特定領域的一部分專家上會導致嚴重的性能下降。這限制了它們在內存受限環境中的實用性，尤其是隨著模型變得更大和更稀疏。我們介紹了EMO，一種設計用於模組化的MoE——專家子集的獨立使用和組合——無需人為定義的先驗。我們的關鍵想法是鼓勵來自相似領域的標記依賴於相似的專家。由於文檔內的標記通常共享一個領域，EMO限制它們從共享池中選擇專家，同時允許不同的文檔使用不同的池。這一簡單的約束使得在僅使用文檔邊界的預訓練過程中出現一致的專家分組。我們在1T標記上預訓練了一個1B活躍、14B總體的EMO。作為完整模型，它的性能與標準MoE相當。關鍵是，它實現了選擇性專家使用：僅保留25%（12.5%）的專家僅會造成1%（3%）的絕對下降，而標準MoE在相同設置下會崩潰。我們進一步發現，EMO中的專家子集在語義層面上專精（例如，數學或代碼等領域），這與標準MoE中觀察到的低級語法專精形成對比。總體而言，我們的結果展示了一條通向模組化、內存高效部署大型稀疏模型的道路，並為可組合架構開辟了新的機會。
2605.06663.author: Ryan Wang et.al.
2605.06663.authors: Ryan Wang, Akshita Bhagia, Sewon Min
